basic_details.job_title: UPSC Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 450 Drug Inspector, Assistant Engineer and More Posts
basic_details.short_information: The Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) has released Advertisement No. 07/2026 on 25 June 2026, inviting online recruitment applications for 54 posts spread across multiple central government ministries and departments. The vacancies include positions such as Drug Inspector (186 posts), Specialist Grade III Assistant Professor across various medical specialities, Aeronautical Officer (11), Legal Officer (12), Company Prosecutor (18), and several others. Eligible candidates can apply online from 25 June 2026 up to 17 July 2026 at 6:00 PM through UPSC's official portal at upsconline.nic.in. T

important_dates.interview_date: To be intimated separately by UPSC

application_fee.details: Important: Applications submitted without the prescribed fee will be summarily rejected. Fee once paid is non-refundable and cannot be held in reserve for any other examination or selection.

age_limit.details: Eligibility requirements for UPSC Advt No. 07/2026 vary significantly by post, as these are specialist and technical positions spanning engineering, law, medicine, and administration. All candidates must be Indian citizens. The closing date for submission of the online application - 17 July 2026 - is the reference date for determining eligibility in every respect. | Age limits differ by post and category. Candidates wondering about age relaxation can note that PwBD candidates receive an additional 10 years of relaxation over and above the prescribed upper age limit, and the maximum age for PwBD candidates shall not exceed 56 years in any case. Ex-Servicemen and Central Government servants are also entitled to age relaxation as per central government rules. | Calculate your exact in-hand Salary in 10 seconds - Calculate Now | OBC candidates: 3 years relaxation over UR upper age limit. SC/ST candidates: 5 years relaxation over UR upper age limit. PwBD (UR): 10 years; PwBD (OBC): 13 years; PwBD (SC/ST): 15 years. Maximum age cap: 56 years. Ex-Servicemen: 5 years relaxation, subject to the condition that continuous service rendered is not less than 6 months after attestation as on the closing date. Central/UT Government Servants: Up to 5 years, subject to 3 years continuous regular service in the same line or allied cadre.

qualification.details: Eligibility requirements for UPSC Advt No. 07/2026 vary significantly by post, as these are specialist and technical positions spanning engineering, law, medicine, and administration. All candidates must be Indian citizens. The closing date for submission of the online application - 17 July 2026 - is the reference date for determining eligibility in every respect.

salary_details.details: Selected candidates will receive pay as per the 7th Central Pay Commission Pay Matrix. The pay level ranges from Level-7 for posts such as Company Prosecutor and Photographic Officer to Level-11 (with NPA) for medical Specialist Grade III posts. In addition to basic pay, candidates are entitled to Dearness Allowance, HRA, and other admissible allowances as per Central Government rules.

selection_process: Online Application Submission: Submit application at upsconline.nic.in by 17 July 2026. Shortlisting: UPSC shortlists candidates on the basis of information provided in the online application. Incorrect information may lead to rejection through computer-based shortlisting. Recruitment Test (RT) / Combined Recruitment Test (CRT) – if applicable: For posts where the number of applications is large
UPSC may conduct an RT or CRT before the interview. The test is ordinarily conducted at the following centres: Ahmedabad

and Visakhapatnam. Personality Test / Interview: Shortlisted candidates are called for interview. Candidates must bring the printout of their online application and all original/self-attested copies of supporting documents. Final Selection and Appointment: Appointment orders are issued by the respective Ministry/Department. UPSC may grant higher initial pay to candidates adjudged meritorious at the interview stage.

content_sections.heading: UPSC Advertisement 07/2026 – Important Dates
content_sections.text: The UPSC Recruitment 2026 Advt. No. 07 notification was released on 25 June 2026. The online application window opened the same day and closes on 17 July 2026 at 1800 hours . The date for determining eligibility for all candidates in every respect is the closing date for submission of the online application.

content_sections.heading: UPSC Advertisement 07/2026 – Eligibility Criteria, Age Limit & Qualifications
content_sections.text: Eligibility requirements for UPSC Advt No. 07/2026 vary significantly by post, as these are specialist and technical positions spanning engineering, law, medicine, and administration. All candidates must be Indian citizens. The closing date for submission of the online application - 17 July 2026 - is the reference date for determining eligibility in every respect. Age limits differ by post and category. Candidates wondering about age relaxation can note that PwBD candidates receive an additional 10 years of relaxation over and above the prescribed upper age limit, and the maximum age for PwBD candidates shall not exceed 56 years in any case. Ex-Servicemen and Central Government servants are also entitled to age relaxation as per central government rules. Calculate your exact in-hand Salary in 10 seconds - Calculate Now OBC candidates: 3 years relaxation over UR upper age limit. SC/ST candidates: 5 years relaxation over UR upper age limit. PwBD (UR): 10 years; PwBD (OBC): 13 years; PwBD (SC/ST): 15 years. Maximum age cap: 56 years. Ex-Servicemen: 5 years relaxation, subject to the condition that continuous service rendered is not less than 6 months after attestation as on the closing date. Central/UT Government Servants: Up to 5 years, subject to 3 years continuous regular service in the same line or allied cadre.

rows.essential_qualification_educational: Master's Degree in Agricultural Economics / Agricultural Extension / Agronomy / Entomology / Nematology / Genetics & Plant Breeding / Agriculture Botany / Plant Bio Technology / Plant Pathology / Plant Physiology / Seeds Science & Technology / Soil Science & Agricultural Chemistry from a recognised university OR Bachelor's Degree in Agricultural Engineering
rows.experience_required: 3 years in production/productivity/planning/development/extension in food or cash crops in a govt. / semi-govt. / PSU / autonomous body / recognised research institute / agricultural university

rows.essential_qualification_educational: Degree in Siddha from a recognised university/statutory state board/faculty of Indian Medicine as recognised under the Indian Medicine Central Council Act, 1970; AND Enrolment on the central or state register of Indian Medicine
Degree in Aeronautical, Electrical, Electronics, Mechanical, or Metallurgical Engineering from a recognised university
rows.experience_required: 2 years in aircraft design and development including aircraft electrical/electronics/mechanical systems or airworthiness engineering

rows.essential_qualification_educational: Degree in Law from a recognised university
rows.experience_required: As specified in the notification (legal practice/experience)

rows.essential_qualification_educational: Degree in Law from a recognised university

rows.post: Specialist Grade III (Medical posts – Ministry of Health)
rows.essential_qualification_educational: MBBS + postgraduate medical degree (MD/MS/DNB) in the relevant speciality from a recognised institution; Registration with MCI/NMC
rows.experience_required: Post-qualification experience in the relevant speciality as prescribed per post

rows.essential_qualification_educational: Degree in Pharmacy or Pharmaceutical Sciences or Medicine with specialisation in Clinical Pharmacology or Microbiology from a recognised university
rows.experience_required: As per the Drugs & Cosmetics Act requirements

rows.essential_qualification_educational: Degree in Civil Engineering from a recognised university

content_sections.heading: UPSC Advt No. 07/2026 Application Fee
content_sections.text: Important: Applications submitted without the prescribed fee will be summarily rejected . Fee once paid is non-refundable and cannot be held in reserve for any other examination or selection.

content_sections.heading: UPSC Recruitment 2026 (Advt 07) – Selection Process
content_sections.text: Online Application Submission: Submit application at upsconline.nic.in by 17 July 2026. Shortlisting: UPSC shortlists candidates on the basis of information provided in the online application. Incorrect information may lead to rejection through computer-based shortlisting. Recruitment Test (RT) / Combined Recruitment Test (CRT) – if applicable: For posts where the number of applications is large, UPSC may conduct an RT or CRT before the interview. The test is ordinarily conducted at the following centres: Ahmedabad, Bhopal, Chennai, Delhi, Dispur (Guwahati), Jaipur, Jammu, Kochi, Kolkata, Lucknow, Mumbai, Nagpur, Port Blair, Ranchi, and Visakhapatnam. Personality Test / Interview: Shortlisted candidates are called for interview. Candidates must bring the printout of their online application and all original/self-attested copies of supporting documents. Final Selection and Appointment: Appointment orders are issued by the respective Ministry/Department. UPSC may grant higher initial pay to candidates adjudged meritorious at the interview stage.

content_sections.heading: UPSC Advt 07/2026 – Salary & Pay Scale (7th CPC Pay Matrix)
content_sections.text: Selected candidates will receive pay as per the 7th Central Pay Commission Pay Matrix. The pay level ranges from Level-7 for posts such as Company Prosecutor and Photographic Officer to Level-11 (with NPA) for medical Specialist Grade III posts. In addition to basic pay, candidates are entitled to Dearness Allowance, HRA, and other admissible allowances as per Central Government rules.

rows.post: Specialist Grade III (all medical speciality posts, Ministry of Health)

content_sections.heading: UPSC Advertisement 07/2026 Apply Online – Step-by-Step Guide
content_sections.text: The UPSC online recruitment application for Advt No. 07/2026 must be submitted through the official UPSC ORA (Online Recruitment Application) portal at upsconline.nic.in . The last date to apply online is 17 July 2026 at 6:00 PM (1800 Hrs) . Candidates are advised to submit their application well in advance to avoid last-minute technical difficulties.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for UPSC Advt 07/2026 – Interview Stage
content_sections.text: Candidates are not required to send any documents to UPSC at the time of application submission. However, all shortlisted candidates called for the interview must carry original documents and self-attested photocopies. Failure to produce required documents at the interview stage will result in disqualification and forfeiture of travelling allowance reimbursement.

content_sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid – UPSC Advt 07/2026 Application
content_sections.text: Applying for a post you do not meet the exact qualification for: UPSC shortlists strictly on the basis of declared qualifications. Applying with a "similar" or "equivalent" degree without a formal equivalence order from the competent authority is grounds for rejection. Produce the equivalence order if claiming an equivalent qualification. Entering an inactive or incorrect e-mail address: UPSC sends all interview schedules and document requirements exclusively by e-mail. A wrong email means you will miss the interview call entirely. Double-check before submitting. Submitting incomplete category documents later: All category-based claims (caste, disability, ex-serviceman) must be supportable at the interview stage with original documents in the prescribed UPSC proforma - not general certificates from hospitals or district offices. Not declaring government service: Persons already in regular government service must submit a declaration to UPSC that they have informed their Head of Office in writing. Omitting this can lead to disciplinary consequences. Attempting to withdraw the application after submission: UPSC explicitly states that candidates will not be allowed to withdraw their application once submitted. Apply only if you are genuinely eligible and intend to participate. Bringing a mobile phone to the exam/interview venue: Mobile phones and all electronic devices are strictly banned at UPSC examination/interview halls - even in switched-off mode. Violation results in debarment from future examinations. Waiting until the last day: The portal closes at exactly 1800 Hrs on 17 July 2026. Server congestion near the deadline is common. Complete your application at least 2–3 days in advance. Submitting documents in a language other than Hindi/English without a notarised transcript: Any certificate in a regional language must be accompanied by a transcript attested by a Gazetted Officer or Notary.

faq.question: Q1. What is UPSC Advertisement No. 07/2026?
faq.answer: UPSC Advertisement No. 07/2026 is a recruitment notification released on 25 June 2026 by the Union Public Service Commission inviting online applications for 54 posts (450 vacancies) across multiple central government ministries and departments, including Drug Inspector, Specialist Grade III medical posts, Aeronautical Officer, Legal Officer, and others.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for UPSC Advt No. 07/2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the Online Recruitment Application is 17 July 2026 at 6:00 PM (1800 Hrs) through the UPSC ORA portal at upsconline.nic.in.

faq.question: Q3. How many total vacancies are there in UPSC Advt 07/2026?
faq.answer: The advertisement covers 54 posts. The largest single block of vacancies is Drug Inspector with 186 posts under CDSCO, followed by medical Specialist Grade III posts across multiple specialities. The total vacancy count across all 54 posts exceeds 487.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for UPSC Advertisement 07/2026?
faq.answer: The application fee is ₹25 for General/OBC/EWS male candidates. Female candidates of all categories, SC, ST, and Persons with Benchmark Disability (PwBD) candidates are fully exempted from fee payment. Fee can be paid via debit/credit card, UPI, or internet banking.

faq.question: Q5. What is the selection process for UPSC Advt 07/2026?
faq.answer: The selection process includes shortlisting of applications, followed by a Recruitment Test (RT) or Combined Recruitment Test (CRT) if required, and then a Personality Test (Interview). Not all posts necessarily require a written test — the interview is the primary selection stage for most posts in this advertisement.

faq.question: Q6. What is the age limit for UPSC Advt No. 07/2026?
faq.answer: Age limits vary by post. For example, the age limit is 40 years (UR) for Joint Director and 35 years (UR) for Medical Officer (Siddha), Aeronautical Officer, and Legal Officer. Reserved category candidates receive standard relaxations: +3 years for OBC, +5 years for SC/ST, and +10 years for PwBD over the UR upper age limit.

faq.question: Q7. Where is the UPSC Advt 07/2026 apply online link?
faq.answer: Applications must be submitted online at the UPSC ORA portal: upsconline.nic.in/ora/. Candidates must register and create a URN profile before applying.

faq.question: Q8. What is the pay scale for posts in UPSC Advertisement 07/2026?
faq.answer: Pay scales are as per the 7th CPC Pay Matrix. Medical Specialist Grade III posts carry Level-11 plus NPA, Aeronautical Officer and Legal Officer carry Level-10, and Company Prosecutor, Photographic Officer, Civilian Assistant Security Officer, and Store Officer carry Level-7.

faq.question: Q9. Is it mandatory to carry documents to the UPSC interview?
faq.answer: Yes. Candidates called for interview must carry the printout of their online application along with all original documents and self-attested copies. Failure to present required documents at the interview will result in disqualification and forfeiture of any travelling allowance reimbursement.

faq.question: Q10. Can I apply for UPSC Advertisement 07/2026 if I am already in government service?
faq.answer: Yes, government employees can apply. However, those already in regular government service (permanent or temporary) must submit a declaration that they have informed their Head of Office/Department in writing that they have applied for this recruitment. They should also note that their application must be submitted directly online to UPSC — not through their department.
